name: Derek Kilmer
subject: The fight for gender equality begins and ends with ALL of us
disclaimer_text: Paid for by People for Derek Kilmer
body:
March is Women's History Month, and today, we celebrate International Women's Day.
[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59875563/395356351/-2027002553?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDMzNzUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMjBmNGE1OWYtZTBiZC1lZDExLWE4ZTAtMDAyMjQ4MzJlODExIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=hW7IfdlnEvAOvO_zb2G43fmBPsKcZB1Ui-Hfr9j59SE=&emci=57aab85c-cdbd-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=20f4a59f-e0bd-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=3595835)

Peter,

March is Women's History Month, and today, we celebrate International Women's Day.

Fairness and equality are core American values. Women have led the charge to ensure our government lives up to the ideals enshrined in the Constitution.

**We stand on the shoulders of American women who have led the fight for justice since before our nation was born. Thanks to their sacrifices and hard work, we have come a long way in the fight for gender equality._Still, our work is not yet over._**

Last year, an extremist-packed Supreme Court stripped away years of settled law by overturning _Roe v. Wade_ – which granted women the legal right to bodily autonomy and the right to choose whether or not to have an abortion.

Peter, I want to be clear: _None of us will be truly equal until all of us are_ — no matter our race, socioeconomic background, **or gender**. That's why I won't stop fighting until these fundamental rights are protected.

Gender equality means equal pay for equal work. It means strong paid leave. It means greenlighting common-sense anti-discrimination legislation. **And it also means the right to access safe and legal reproductive health care.**

**[So that's why I'm asking: On this International Women's Day, will you join me in recommitting to the fight for equality by adding your name?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59875564/395356352/-98006153?ms=em23_230308_wmsday_enask_gq_act___DK_ettx&refcode=em23_230308_wmsday_enask_gq_act___DK_ettx&source=em23_230308_wmsday_enask_gq_act___DK_ettx&utm_campaign=em23_230308_wmsday_enask_gq_act___DK_ettx&utm_medium=email&contactdata=eha1vZN6SQcsormQYVmqraQMGgGurdfwFcdg2dSVsemJtmbzALGFr/Qom2xxzaeiF2EAiuiHwIQ2n/gCAsmYgMio4+IqeivB8Os5xPIPEvYQXZUPv5yyp8cL7VzXliXOJvp1ClqYmDOr5Scf/LqiRdv14uyEmRtHEN/NCtPfXnU=&refcode2=20f4a59f-e0bd-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDMzNzUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMjBmNGE1OWYtZTBiZC1lZDExLWE4ZTAtMDAyMjQ4MzJlODExIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=hW7IfdlnEvAOvO_zb2G43fmBPsKcZB1Ui-Hfr9j59SE=&emci=57aab85c-cdbd-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=20f4a59f-e0bd-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=3595835)**

clean_body: March is Women's History Month, and today, we celebrate International Women's Day. Peter, March is Women's History Month, and today, we celebrate International Women's Day. Fairness and equality are core American values. Women have led the charge to ensure our government lives up to the ideals enshrined in the Constitution. We stand on the shoulders of American women who have led the fight for justice since before our nation was born. Thanks to their sacrifices and hard work, we have come a long way in the fight for gender equality.Still, our work is not yet over. Last year, an extremist-packed Supreme Court stripped away years of settled law by overturning Roe v. Wade – which granted women the legal right to bodily autonomy and the right to choose whether or not to have an abortion. Peter, I want to be clear: None of us will be truly equal until all of us are — no matter our race, socioeconomic background, or gender. That's why I won't stop fighting until these fundamental rights are protected. Gender equality means equal pay for equal work. It means strong paid leave. It means greenlighting common-sense anti-discrimination legislation. And it also means the right to access safe and legal reproductive health care. Thanks for your support. In solidarity, Derek Paid for by People for Derek Kilmer People for Derek Kilmer PO Box 1381 Tacoma, WA 98402 United States Peter, thank you so much for being a supporter of Derek Kilmer and Democrats up and down the ballot. | =

name: Amy Klobuchar
subject: No one expected me to run for elected office
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rosen for Nevada
body:
and not many people expected me to win either
| | |
---
Friend, I want to tell you a little bit about why fighting for American families in the U.S. Senate is so important to me. **[_My work is made possible by this grassroots team so, if you’re able, I hope you split a contribution of any amount between myself and Jacky Rosen to help me keep going._](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59892516/395401109/-170615241?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk2401-bu-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwN2I2NTNkYS0wOWJlLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=b8RgbbtOwiTRd-o0NROr6qpYBP1valFXC_bDe2LBgjk=)**
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59892517/395401110/-170615241?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk2401-bu-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwN2I2NTNkYS0wOWJlLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=b8RgbbtOwiTRd-o0NROr6qpYBP1valFXC_bDe2LBgjk=)
Every day, I’m reminded of the fact that I stand on the shoulders of those before me. I am the daughter of a newspaperman and a teacher and the granddaughter of an iron ore miner who saved his money in a coffee can in the basement to send my dad to college. No one expected me to run for elected office -- and not many people expected me to win either.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59892518/395401111/-170615241?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk2401-bu-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwN2I2NTNkYS0wOWJlLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=b8RgbbtOwiTRd-o0NROr6qpYBP1valFXC_bDe2LBgjk=)
**But thanks to supporters like you, our grassroots movement beat the odds, and I became the first woman elected to the U.S. Senate from Minnesota._That’s the American dream in action. _**

Every day since then I’ve been fighting for progress. And I want you to know:

**I will never stop fighting to build a nation that works for working families. **
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59892519/395401112/-170615241?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk2401-bu-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwN2I2NTNkYS0wOWJlLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=b8RgbbtOwiTRd-o0NROr6qpYBP1valFXC_bDe2LBgjk=)
**[_But I can’t do it alone, friend. That's why I'm so thankful to have grassroots supporters like you by my side. If you can, I’m asking you to split a quick donation between myself and Jacky today to help me continue this fight._](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59892520/395401113/-170615241?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk2401-bu-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwN2I2NTNkYS0wOWJlLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=b8RgbbtOwiTRd-o0NROr6qpYBP1valFXC_bDe2LBgjk=)**
Thank you so much -- this team has never let me down.

\-- Amy

name: Katie Porter
subject: Beer, Bourbon, Single Moms
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Senate
body:
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/14558?t=1&akid=19005%2E3422442%2EJPlq_5)
This International Women's Day, I’m thinking about _why_ we need the voices of women in government. This fact paints a pretty clear picture: **When I headed to Congressional orientation in 2018, there was a Beer Caucus and a Bourbon Caucus, but I was the only single-mom of school aged kids.**
This year, a record number of women are serving, but still the U.S. House is made up of **29% women and the Senate just 25%**. It’s no coincidence the issues of women and families have been put on the backburner for decades.
**Women, myself included, bring a different lived experience to Congress than most of our male colleagues:** I’ve made affordable childcare one of my top priorities in Congress, knowing how most families struggle to find reliable childcare that fits into their budget—because I’ve been there.
After the Supreme Court overturned _Roe v. Wade,_ I thought about how my own daughter has less autonomy than I did growing up.
As our country has recovered from the economic fallout of the pandemic, I’ve been a fierce advocate for making sure women are included in the recovery, after they left the workforce in record numbers.
I’m proud to be from a state with a history of electing trailblazing women to the United States Senate—Dianne Feinstein, Barbara Boxer, and Kamala Harris. These leaders have carved a path that women in politics across the country continue to follow, myself included.
**[Now, I need your help to become the next Senator from California. In the Senate I’ll continue fighting for an economy that works for working people and a government that respects people’s rights. Can you pitch in today?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-20230308-1?refcode=EMAF08_1_WOM_20230308_V1_F&refcode2=19005_3422442_JPlq_5&akid=19005%2E3422442%2EJPlq_5)**
**[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-20230308-1?amount=5&refcode=EMAF08_1_WOM_20230308_V1_F&refcode2=19005_3422442_JPlq_5&akid=19005%2E3422442%2EJPlq_5)**
Thanks for your support, Derek, Happy International Women’s Day!

Katie Porter

Katie is running for the U.S. Senate because Californians deserve a warrior fighting for them in Washington. Our campaign is people-powered, we don’t take a dime from corporate PACs, federal lobbyists, or executives from Big Oil or Big Pharma. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-footer?refcode=EMAF08_1_WOM_20230308_V1_F&t=2&refcode2=19005_3422442_JPlq_5&akid=19005%2E3422442%2EJPlq_5)**

Katie Porter for Senate

clean_body: This International Women's Day, I’m thinking about why we need the voices of women in government. This fact paints a pretty clear picture: When I headed to Congressional orientation in 2018, there was a Beer Caucus and a Bourbon Caucus, but I was the only single-mom of school aged kids. This year, a record number of women are serving, but still the U.S. House is made up of 29% women and the Senate just 25%. It’s no coincidence the issues of women and families have been put on the backburner for decades. Women, myself included, bring a different lived experience to Congress than most of our male colleagues: I’ve made affordable childcare one of my top priorities in Congress, knowing how most families struggle to find reliable childcare that fits into their budget—because I’ve been there. After the Supreme Court overturned Roe v. Wade, I thought about how my own daughter has less autonomy than I did growing up. As our country has recovered from the economic fallout of the pandemic, I’ve been a fierce advocate for making sure women are included in the recovery, after they left the workforce in record numbers. I’m proud to be from a state with a history of electing trailblazing women to the United States Senate—Dianne Feinstein, Barbara Boxer, and Kamala Harris. These leaders have carved a path that women in politics across the country continue to follow, myself included. Thanks for your support, Derek, Happy International Women’s Day! Katie Porter Katie is running for the U.S. Senate because Californians deserve a warrior fighting for them in Washington. Our campaign is people-powered, we don’t take a dime from corporate PACs, federal lobbyists, or executives from Big Oil or Big Pharma.

name: Harmeet Dhillon
subject: Help us stop gender experiments on children
body:
| |
---
| | Fellow Patriot, Chloe Cole was just 13 years old when activist doctors decided she was "trans"... _... and fast-tracked her into Kaiser Permanente 's multi-billion-dollar __industry to "transition" her from a girl to a boy._
---
| [](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62410497:6ENpUaGWN:m:1:3331421248:CE7EAF13C9CA7CCFCD6709A72445AE35:r)
---
| |
* She was given weekly testosterone injections.
* She was injected with an experimental drug used to chemically castrate sex offenders.
* And finally, at age 15, the doctors at Kaiser Permanente removed Chloe's healthy breasts in a radical double mastectomy.

---
| | Chloe and her parents were coerced by these doctors into thinking she was a boy. They purposefully lied to her about the side effects of the experimental treatments they were forcing her under. **And they effectively used Chloe as a lab rat to "test" their "gender affirming care."** Now for the rest of Chloe's life, her voice will always be deeper, she'll never be able to breastfeed - if she can even have children - and who knows what cancers, fertility problems, cardiac problems, bone problems, and more lurk in her future. **Bottom line: The doctors at Kaiser Permanente destroyed Chloe 's healthy body for profit.** **And this is happening to children across America.** My non-profit, the Center for American Liberty, is helping Chloe Cole SUE Kaiser Permanente for permanently destroying her body... **... and finally bring justice to those who have suffered the same fate at the hands of doctors they should have been able to trust. **
---
| | |
---
**I 'm asking you to support Chloe's landmark lawsuit today by contributing a tax-deductible donation to the case's legal fund.**
---
[**DONATE AND HELP SUPPORT CHLOE**](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62410497:6ENpUaGWN:m:1:3331421248:CE7EAF13C9CA7CCFCD6709A72445AE35:r)
---
|
---
| | Winning this case won't be easy. Kaiser Permanente is the largest medical group in the country. They're going to come at us with high-powered lawyers to protect their goldmine. **But making radical activists like this pay through litigation is the only way we 'll shut them down.**
---
| | |
---
**I 'm counting on you to join me in Chloe's fight today by supporting our legal battle with a tax-deductible donation to our legal fund.**
---
[**DONATE AND HELP SUPPORT CHLOE**](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/62410497:6ENpUaGWN:m:1:3331421248:CE7EAF13C9CA7CCFCD6709A72445AE35:r)
---
Any donation you can send today will get us one step closer to finally bringing Chloe the justice she deserves after being coerced and harmed by a hospital that abused her like a lab rat.
---

name: Cori Bush HQ
subject: Action on the Housing Crisis
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cori Bush for Congress
body:
Cori is co-chairing the Caucus on Homelessness.
#
**This month, Cori alongside three of her colleagues in Congress reestablished The Congressional Caucus on Homelessness.** Cori actually knows _firsthand_ what it’s like to live unhoused, and her perspective and passion on this issue make her an indispensable advocate in the Caucus. Her experience will be a crucial component to getting impactful policies passed in the House.
**We know this country doesn't lack the resources needed to fix this problem — and soon, with Cori’s leadership — Congress won’t lack the political will, either.[Will you help us amplify her voice on the housing crisis with a contribution of $5?](https://act.coribush.org/go/238?c0=cori-em-20230308.speW1y&refcode=em20230308-2298&t=1&akid=2298%2E1082990%2E2uv6dB)**
**The Congressional Caucus on Homelessness will:**
* provide a dedicated forum for Members of Congress to work toward the goal of ending the unhoused crisis in this country;
* analyze the layered and complex factors contributing to homelessness;
* educate members on the various federal programs vital to serving our unhoused community members; and importantly,
* advocate for funding for programs and policies necessary to secure housing and life-changing services for the nation’s 580,000 individuals experiencing homelessness in the United States.

**[Will you make a donation of $5 and help us amplify Cori’s voice on housing insecurity and generate public support for the dignified, people-first solutions to this crisis that our unhoused neighbors deserve?](https://act.coribush.org/go/238?c0=cori-em-20230308.speW1y&refcode=em20230308-2298&t=2&akid=2298%2E1082990%2E2uv6dB)**
**[Chip in $5](https://act.coribush.org/go/238?c0=cori-em-20230308.speW1y&refcode=em20230308-2298&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=21&akid=2298%2E1082990%2E2uv6dB)**
Right now, it’s estimated that 580,000 individuals are unhoused in this country. **Our policies are failing 580,000 people each and every day — and Congress has a moral responsibility to take real action.** The need for housing is universal and Cori’s confident that with a forum to cultivate ideas and drive solutions — we can ultimately solve the housing crisis for _everyone._
Thank you,
Cori Bush HQ

**[Contribute ››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cori-em-20230308?refcode=em20230308-2298-footer&t=22&refcode2=2298_1082990_2uv6dB&akid=2298%2E1082990%2E2uv6dB) **
---
Follow us on **[Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/VoteCoriBush/)** , **[Twitter](https://twitter.com/CoriBush)** , **[Instagram](https://www.instagram.com/coribush/)** , and **[visit our website](https://coribush.org/)**. This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Mail us: Cori Bush for Congress, 75 North Oaks Plaza, St. Louis, MO 63121.

name: AG Miyares
subject: cause for celebration 🎉

50 years ago, my mother escaped Cuba and Fidel Castro’s socialist regime with just the clothes on her back. By the grace of God, she overcame unimaginable odds to become an American. Because of her courage, I went to school, became a prosecutor, served in the Virginia House of Delegates, and made history by becoming Virginia’s first Latino-American Attorney General. **Now, the radical socialist ideas my mother escaped in Cuba are uprooting our laws and freedoms, but I will never stop fighting to protect the American Dream.**
---

It’s a time to honor the work women do every day to create a more just world. Today, we’d like to continue our celebration of Black History Month by showing our deep appreciation for a few of the amazing Black women who have paved the way for the voting rights movement in Georgia. They’ve marched, they've taken the microphone, they’ve testified, and they’ve even run for office. Take a look for yourself at how these changemakers have been leading the way and making history: **Helen Butler**
When it comes to engaging voters in Georgia, voting rights activist Helen Butler has been in the fight for decades. As Executive Director at the Georgia Coalition for the People’s Agenda, her work continues to bring together community organizers, policy experts, faith leaders, and more to improve the livelihoods of people of color in Georgia and across the country. She’s a pillar in her community, dedicated to building coalitions and ensuring voter participation in communities of color. **LaTosha Brown**
An award-winning organizer, strategist, and activist, LaTosha Brown is the Co-Founder of Black Voters Matter, an organization dedicated to increasing power in historically marginalized, predominantly Black neighborhoods. In Georgia, they led year-round organizing efforts to help turn out record numbers of voters in 2020 and have led grassroots campaigns across 14 states. **Taifa Smith Butler**
Taifa Smith Butler spent nearly a decade leading the Georgia Budget and Policy Institute, with a mission of identifying lasting solutions that improve economic opportunity for all Georgians. She worked at the intersection of a number of issues, calling out policies like Georgia’s fines and fees revenue practices and how they systematically disenfranchise and overtax Black and brown voters. **Stacey Abrams**
Voting rights champion and founder of Fair Fight Action, Stacey Abrams has been a longtime advocate fighting to make voting accessible for all eligible voters. Her work has been centered around making the voting process inclusive and fighting to ensure the integrity of our voting process and our democracy. After serving as House Minority Leader in the Georgia legislature, she ran for Governor of Georgia and brought national attention to systemic voter suppression prevalent throughout the state. Her persistence in this movement, exemplified by the founding of Fair Fight Action, inspires us to keep up the fight. The strides we’ve made in protecting the freedom to vote in Georgia — inspiring progress in the rest of the country — would not have been possible without the work of these brave activists. While we have so much work left to do to combat anti-democratic extremism, we know with you on our team, we’ll keep making history. In solidarity, The Fair Fight Action Team | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59882039/395362125/-972217868?refcode=bsc_emm_ffa_20230308_e_iwd_v1s1&ms=bsc_emm_ffa_20230308_e_iwd_v1s1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5Zjg1ZDRlYy1lM2JkLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=xF3DncyoiNjVfOnTcsw9ncabyw_XtpXj6Hzhlr5Wj84=)

name: Judicial Watch Corruption Chronicles
subject: DHS Invites Communist State Sponsor of Terrorism to Tour U.S. Maritime Security Facilities
body:
As if the Mexican border crisis were not sufficiently compromising
national security, the Biden administration invited officials from a
communist country that appears on the government’s list of state
terrorism sponsors to tour sensitive U.S. maritime security
facilities.

national security, the Biden administration invited officials from a
communist country that appears on the government’s list of state
terrorism sponsors to tour sensitive U.S. maritime security
facilities. The bizarre secret jaunt was scheduled last week to grant
a delegation from Cuba’s Border Guard and Ministry of Foreign
Affairs access to the nation’s Coast Guard Headquarters in
Washington D.C. as well as port facilities in Wilmington, North
Carolina.

**Here's why this race is important:** Lauren Boebert is as bad as the GOP gets. [ CNN’s primetime special featuring First Lady Jill Biden, called “Jill Biden Abroad,” failed to attract viewers and lost 43% of CNN’s average 2023 9 p.m. ET viewership. The special averaged only 368,000 total viewers, while Fox News’s “Hannity” and MSNBC’s “The Rachel Maddow Show” drew 2.7 million and 2.3 million viewers, respectively. ](https://click.americanupdate.com/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=01%7c651391%7c210334%7c805%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=uGKba3Hu0UIYVn4y5vgCQg&sysid=1)

name: Hannah Ledford, Campaigns Director
subject: 1 year ago today
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Fairness Project The Fairness Project
body:
We went on offense to protect abortion — and won. | [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/59883311/395363923/765663662?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRlAvMS84NTQ3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhNmMxNmE3NC1lNWJkLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=zqt27KRARwyFeuDoojuqev8ClUaFhACJYYkt_-OChlY=&emci=bb0bd44b-cbbd-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=a6c16a74-e5bd-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=561582) | Friend — 1 year ago, our team at the Fairness Project stepped into uncharted waters alongside our partners in Michigan to launch the **first-ever ballot measure campaign to proactively safeguard abortion. ** Michigan was one of _NINE_ states that had an abortion ban on the books when the _Roe v. Wade_ ruling originally came out, _and we knew it was a real possibility that in just a few months an anti-abortion Supreme Court would rule on Dobbs v. Jackson, ending decades of precedent by overturning Roe v. Wade — putting this ban back into effect._ **We had no choice but to take action to proactively protect abortion rights in Michigan, so alongside the ACLU and Planned Parenthood, we launched a ballot measure campaign.** _Remember this? _ [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/59883312/395363925/-1558224708?refcode=E20522-20230308-RFFA1Year-graphic-Other-RegTemplate-NA-NA&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRlAvMS84NTQ3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhNmMxNmE3NC1lNWJkLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=zqt27KRARwyFeuDoojuqev8ClUaFhACJYYkt_-OChlY=) [POWER MORE CAMPAIGNS LIKE THIS ONE →](https://click.everyaction.com/k/59883313/395363927/-1558224708?refcode=E20522-20230308-RFFA1Year-SB-Other-RegTemplate-NA-NA&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRlAvMS84NTQ3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhNmMxNmE3NC1lNWJkLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=zqt27KRARwyFeuDoojuqev8ClUaFhACJYYkt_-OChlY=) Over the course of the next 8 months, voters across all party lines from every single corner of Michigan rallied behind this movement and fought hard to preserve their reproductive freedom. _**And our history-making campaign broke records, team.**_ 753,759 signatures were submitted to qualify this measure to appear on the ballot in November — almost DOUBLE the amount required, _and the most ever submitted in Michigan history!_ And on Election Day, Michigan voters from all walks of life came together and decisively voted to restore their right to reproductive freedom once and for all. clean_body: We went on offense to protect abortion — and won. | | Friend — 1 year ago, our team at the Fairness Project stepped into uncharted waters alongside our partners in Michigan to launch the first-ever ballot measure campaign to proactively safeguard abortion. Michigan was one of NINE states that had an abortion ban on the books when the Roe v. Wade ruling originally came out, and we knew it was a real possibility that in just a few months an anti-abortion Supreme Court would rule on Dobbs v. Jackson, ending decades of precedent by overturning Roe v. Wade — putting this ban back into effect. We had no choice but to take action to proactively protect abortion rights in Michigan, so alongside the ACLU and Planned Parenthood, we launched a ballot measure campaign. Remember this? = = Over the course of the next 8 months, voters across all party lines from every single corner of Michigan rallied behind this movement and fought hard to preserve their reproductive freedom. And our history-making campaign broke records, team. 753,759 signatures were submitted to qualify this measure to appear on the ballot in November — almost DOUBLE the amount required, and the most ever submitted in Michigan history! And on Election Day, Michigan voters from all walks of life came together and decisively voted to restore their right to reproductive freedom once and for all. We’re proud of every single campaign we’ve run, friend. But we are especially proud of this one. At a time when it felt like SCOTUS and extremist officials across the country left us stranded without reproductive health care access, this campaign proved that ballot measures could be the last line of defense to preserve our most personal freedoms. = | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to the Fairness Project will go through immediately: | = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Thank you for your support, Hannah Ledford Campaigns Director Fairness Project Thanks to grassroots supporters like you, our team at the Fairness Project has won 31 people-powered ballot measure campaigns since 2016, changing over 18 million lives by expanding health care, raising wages, guaranteeing paid leave, and ending predatory lending directly at the ballot box when politicians refuse to act. Your support allows us to make this progress possible.** = = Contributions or gifts to the Fairness Project are not deductible as charitable contributions for federal income tax purposes. Contributions to the Fairness Project support its many efforts nationwide and any focus is at the Fairness Project’s sole discretion. Contributions will not necessarily be used to support activity in any one state. You can request to get only the most important emails from our team , or you can . Because March 31st marks the end of the fundraising quarter. When the deadline passes, our fundraising figures go public. Liberals and their media allies will be looking for any sign of weakness — and it’s critical that we don’t give it to them. https://winred.kansansformarshall.com/marshall/2023-march-eoq?utm_sourc e=sgrc&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=2023march8hf If you’re able, I’m asking you to chip in $23 — or even just $8 — right now to ensure we stay on track. While Senate Democrats have the full support of elites and big corporations, I depend on small dollar donors like you. Derek, Across the globe, it’s International Women’s Day—a day meant to reflect on the achievements and contributions of women in society, but also to ensure greater equity and equality. That means coming together to ensure equal educational opportunities, equal pay, and equal access to healthcare—especially reproductive care. Thankfully, our congresswomen from Virginia are in Washington, busy doing just that: fighting tooth and nail for the rights of every woman, everywhere. subject: Our pledge to you, Peter
body:
You are an invaluable part of our grassroots movement, and your time, energy, and investments in our campaign are the foundation of our success. | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59893412/395413940/657127102?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDMxMTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZWNlZWMzYmYtMTBiZS1lZDExLWE4ZTAtMDAyMjQ4MzJlODExIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=a6-4EYneRREGL5Rjk47LmcstCH6mxEtOh7GRW1APfA8=) | Peter, You are an invaluable part of our grassroots movement, and your time, energy, and investments in our campaign are the foundation of our success. But too often, candidates and campaigns treat supporters like money machines or ATMs **_— and we think that’s wrong._** Whether you’re a monthly recurring donor, a one-time contributor, or simply a supporter of this campaign, we promise to always treat you like a real person, not a wallet. | **So heading into the 2024 campaign, here is our pledge to you:**
* We will never take a single dime of corporate PAC money
* We will never use a fake emergency or deadline to get you to donate to this campaign
* We will be truthful about the amount of money we raise, our progress, and our goals
* We will never use hidden recurring donations or trick you into donating without your consent

---
Here on Team Harder, we want to make it clear that Josh doesn’t need shady fundraising tactics or corporate PAC money to run a winning campaign.
**Grassroots supporters like YOU are what allow us to run a 100% people-powered movement — and together, we’re strong enough to accomplish anything we set our minds to.**
Thanks again for being a part of our movement,
\- Team Harder

body:
We bet you didn’t. | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59883684/395365607/17860314?refcode=ea.6fb3e0a0-e7bd-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ms=ea.6fb3e0a0-e7bd-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDMzMTAiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNmZiM2UwYTAtZTdiZC1lZDExLWE4ZTAtMDAyMjQ4MzJlODExIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=KIXa3SaLfcS8sUJsl_Pnexet0jWOSdKQz5bXpmJvdcI=)

Some fast facts to know about Brandon Presley:

**1️⃣ He’s a lifelong Mississippian.**

Brandon was born and raised by a single mother in a blue-collar family. He dedicated his life to public service and became mayor of his hometown of Nettleton.

**2️⃣ He knows how to get things done.**

He opened up meetings that had been closed to the public for decades, brought high-speed internet to some of the most remote and forgotten parts of our state, created educational opportunities and jobs, and even saved taxpayers over 6 billion dollars.

**3️⃣ He’ll always stand up for the little guy.**

Brandon is well known for standing up to special interests, corporate lobbyists, utility companies, and anybody else who’s tried to screw over the hardworking people and families of Mississippi.

Brandon is ready to send Governor Tate Reeves packing and to move Mississippi forward. Are you with him?

clean_body: We bet you didn’t. | = Some fast facts to know about Brandon Presley: 1️⃣ He’s a lifelong Mississippian. Brandon was born and raised by a single mother in a blue-collar family. He dedicated his life to public service and became mayor of his hometown of Nettleton. 2️⃣ He knows how to get things done. He opened up meetings that had been closed to the public for decades, brought high-speed internet to some of the most remote and forgotten parts of our state, created educational opportunities and jobs, and even saved taxpayers over 6 billion dollars. 3️⃣ He’ll always stand up for the little guy. Brandon is well known for standing up to special interests, corporate lobbyists, utility companies, and anybody else who’s tried to screw over the hardworking people and families of Mississippi. Brandon is ready to send Governor Tate Reeves packing and to move Mississippi forward. Are you with him? = | = | = | = ---|---|--- = | = | = Thank you for your support, Team Presley | | = = Follow along: Paid for by Brandon Presley Campaign Brandon Presley Campaign P.O. name: Wes Moore
subject: Share our petition to protect reproductive choice →
body:
| [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59884181/395366768/-926250309?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDQ1NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZmJhYjQ4Ni1lOWJkLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=3dXruU_X8fOVKKstQH4aBy25ZUh1xlLJfUTZ2BXriLk=&emci=ba505880-e9bd-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=afbab486-e9bd-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=24635485)
---
Friends, Despite years of hard-fought progress, reproductive rights in the United States are under attack like never before. Extreme abortion bans and other bills that roll back access to reproductive care and even criminalize abortion have been introduced in Republican-controlled states across the country, with some moving forward since the federal right to abortion guaranteed in _Roe v. Wade_ was overturned by the Supreme Court last year. As your governor, I am committed to protecting reproductive rights in Maryland and making our state a safe haven for abortion. **That’s why I’ve joined the Reproductive Freedom Alliance, a coalition of 21 Democratic governors working together to protect and expand reproductive rights in our states.** I fully support four bills heading to the floor of our state legislature that will protect abortion access, privacy, and out-of-state patients—including a bill to create a constitutional amendment that will enshrine reproductive rights in the Maryland Constitution. **Will you help us build even more public support for this constitutional amendment to protect choice in Maryland by sharing our petition with your friends and family today? Forward this email to 3 or more people and share on social media:** | [SHARE ON TWITTER](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59884182/395366769/343342365?url=https://support.wesmoore.com/a/reproductive-freedom&text=I%20join%20Governor%20Wes%20Moore%20in%20supporting%20a%20constitutional%20amendment%20to%20guarantee%20the%20right%20to%20abortion%20in%20Maryland.%20Sign%20the%20petition%20if%20you%20do%20too!&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDQ1NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZmJhYjQ4Ni1lOWJkLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=3dXruU_X8fOVKKstQH4aBy25ZUh1xlLJfUTZ2BXriLk=&emci=ba505880-e9bd-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=afbab486-e9bd-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=24635485)
---
[SHARE ON FACEBOOK](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59884183/395366770/989981403?u=https://support.wesmoore.com/a/reproductive-freedom&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDQ1NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZmJhYjQ4Ni1lOWJkLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=3dXruU_X8fOVKKstQH4aBy25ZUh1xlLJfUTZ2BXriLk=&emci=ba505880-e9bd-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=afbab486-e9bd-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=24635485)
---
Marylanders and members of the 20 other coalition states are coming together to stand up against the extreme, dangerous attacks on reproductive freedom. Thank you for being part of our movement.
Elevate,
Wes
**PS —[If you haven’t already, you can sign our petition in support of the constitutional amendment to protect reproductive choice in Maryland here →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59884184/395366771/534357688?refcode=fl_em_sh_20230308alliance_wm_180da_xx_ps&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDQ1NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZmJhYjQ4Ni1lOWJkLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=3dXruU_X8fOVKKstQH4aBy25ZUh1xlLJfUTZ2BXriLk=&emci=ba505880-e9bd-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=afbab486-e9bd-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=24635485)**

name: Hoosier Republicans
subject: A different type of March Madness bracket
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE INDIANA REPUBLICAN STATE COMMITTEE
body:
Make your picks. Read more >>
| | | [](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/f7f15e9875c39250df60ac364a9f256a/40313/61890704cf96989b/d12cd1b1baf2f53fc3d6b838967d376c/40313)
---
Hoosier Republican,

[](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/f7f15e9875c39250df60ac364a9f256a/40313/61890704cf96989b/2d0d2a4b99da7f86c3d6b838967d376c/40313)On Sunday, thousands of Hoosiers will fill out their 2023 NCAA March Madness bracket and make their picks on which teams will make their way to the Final Four in Houston, Texas. **But before you do that, we’ve got a different kind of bracket we’d like you to fill out first —** The Biden administration has made plenty of blunders during its tenure, so we've created a bracket to see which one you think has been the biggest (so far). There were _a lot_ of possibilities, but we've narrowed it down to the following:
* The Chinese spy balloon incident
* The chaos at the southern border
* The decision to cancel the Keystone KL pipeline and beg OPEC for oil
* The record-high inflation
* The massive supply chain issues and empty store shelves
* The botched response to the East Palestine derailment
* The chaotic withdrawal from Afghanistan
* The “End of quote. Repeat the line” gaffe

[**Please, take a second to fill out Round One >>**](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/f7f15e9875c39250df60ac364a9f256a/40313/61890704cf96989b/a268e7634e944224c3d6b838967d376c/40313)

---
[**VOTE NOW >>**](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/f7f15e9875c39250df60ac364a9f256a/40313/61890704cf96989b/d0d1d276de92d3b1c3d6b838967d376c/40313)
---
**We’re closing entry for this bracket in just 48 hours, so please don’t wait!** **We'll send you an email when Round Two opens.** — Hoosier Republicans **PS: While you’re here**, [will you please chip in $5](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/f7f15e9875c39250df60ac364a9f256a/40313/61890704cf96989b/926e78b359fd2b24c3d6b838967d376c/40313)[ or more](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/f7f15e9875c39250df60ac364a9f256a/40313/61890704cf96989b/02f77b9b68282192c3d6b838967d376c/40313) to help us gear up for the crucial election battle we’re facing in 2024? [You can follow this secure link to contribute.](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/f7f15e9875c39250df60ac364a9f256a/40313/61890704cf96989b/44ec5c57906dbdc4c3d6b838967d376c/40313) Thank you!

body:
Now that we're up by 30, it's time to unite...

---
| | | | | | | | Friend,
---
| George Soros said he's hoping the Republican primary drags on forever so that Biden can quietly walk away with the White House unscathed.
---
| _... I wouldn't be shocked if he even meddles in our primary to make that happen!_
---
| **_That 's why, now that we've secured a 30-POINT LEAD, it's time for Republicans to COALESCE and UNITE behind "Trump 2024" so that we can focus 100% on defeating Biden._**
---
| _We need to keep surging EVEN HIGHER in the polls so that there 's NO QUESTION that Primary Voters are _UNITED_ behind "Trump 2024."_
---
| I've set a new goal of 1,000,000 Primary Voters to make a contribution of any amount before 11:59 P.M. TONIGHT.
---

body:
We can’t let this disinformation spread unchallenged
#
Derek,
You might have seen us talking about our campaign to ban lying Fox hosts and their broadcasts on Military facilities.
You might have seen our new video, too.
But Tucker Carlson went ahead and made a better case for this ban than we ever could.
Speaker McCarthy turned over all of the footage from the January 6th Committee to Tucker Carlson. And on Monday night, Tucker used his platform to further the ridiculous idea that the insurrectionists storming the Capitol were peaceful protestors.
What a crock.
We were all watching on January 6th. We all know what we saw.
Smashed in doors. Broken windows. Law enforcement officers beaten with flagpoles bearing the American flag. Veterans lost their lives.
We can’t let this disinformation spread unchallenged, and there is absolutely no place for Tucker or any of his lying colleagues on Military bases.
So how about it, Derek — are you with us?

name: Our Revolution
subject: Ro Khanna’s plan to make sure rail companies don’t keep putting us in danger
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Our Revolution click here to unsubscribe
body:
Peter Willis,

After the catastrophe in East Palestine,
Ohio, it’s clear to anyone paying attention that rail companies must be
appropriately regulated.

Failure to enforce basic environmental and safety protections causes
unnecessary pain for Americans and wreaks havoc on our communities and
environment.

That’s why progressive leader Rep. Ro Khanna and Rep. Chris Deluzio have
introduced a new plan called the DERAIL Act.

Our Revolution is proud to support this legislation to enforce higher
safety standards on the railways.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/signup/Derail_Act/?source=EM_NBI_OR_20230308_PT_DerailAct_0_3MO_OurRevolution_NA_NA_DerailAct&t=1009&akid=4097%2E5885482%2EZEPANT ]Stand against corporate greed in the rail industry by signing on in
support of the DERAIL Act!

The rail industry has used its economic power to exploit its workforce,
deny sick days, and furiously lobby to keep inadequate regulations,
putting workers and communities at risk of toxic exposure and long-term
ecological damage.

The company responsible for the East Palestine catastrophe, Norfolk
Southern, was caught lobbying just three months ago against a bill that
would have required trains to have at least two crew members in case of
emergencies.

Enough is enough; it’s time to take action to protect our safety.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/signup/Derail_Act/?source=EM_NBI_OR_20230308_PT_DerailAct_0_3MO_OurRevolution_NA_NA_DerailAct&t=1010&akid=4097%2E5885482%2EZEPANT ]Add your Name if you agree that rail companies must put our safety
ahead of their shareholder profits!

When we organize, we win.

Our Revolution

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/5923?c0=EM_NBI_OR_20230308_PT_DerailAct_0_3MO_OurRevolution_NA_NA_DerailAct.PEnmTi&t=1001&akid=4097%2E5885482%2EZEPANT ]Our membership card

Our Revolution is the largest entirely grassroots funded progressive

clean_body: Peter Willis, After the catastrophe in East Palestine, Ohio, it’s clear to anyone paying attention that rail companies must be appropriately regulated. Failure to enforce basic environmental and safety protections causes unnecessary pain for Americans and wreaks havoc on our communities and environment. That’s why progressive leader Rep. Ro Khanna and Rep. Chris Deluzio have introduced a new plan called the DERAIL Act. Our Revolution is proud to support this legislation to enforce higher safety standards on the railways. [ ]Stand against corporate greed in the rail industry by signing on in support of the DERAIL Act! The rail industry has used its economic power to exploit its workforce, deny sick days, and furiously lobby to keep inadequate regulations, putting workers and communities at risk of toxic exposure and long-term ecological damage. The company responsible for the East Palestine catastrophe, Norfolk Southern, was caught lobbying just three months ago against a bill that would have required trains to have at least two crew members in case of emergencies. Enough is enough; it’s time to take action to protect our safety. [ ]Add your Name if you agree that rail companies must put our safety ahead of their shareholder profits! When we organize, we win. name: Eric Swalwell
subject: This International Women’s Day, we need to make a few things clear:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Swalwell For Congress
body:
Democrat Eric Swalwell represents California's 14th Congressional District.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ESCA-14-Email-march2023-fr-march?refcode=headerfooter-header&amount=25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ESCA-14-Email-march2023-fr-march?refcode=headerfooter-header&amount=25]
Happy International Women’s Day, friend!

On International Women's Day, we recognize that a balanced world is a better world. But friend, despite the incredible gains we’ve made over the last year, like electing a record number of women to the 118th Congress, we are far from achieving a balanced world.

This year would’ve marked the 50th anniversary of Roe v. Wade, but as we all know, the Supreme Court overturned the landmark 1973 ruling in an overreaching partisan act. Let me make a few things clear:

When women don’t have the freedom to make their own health care decisions, they do not have equal rights.

When women are forced to give birth without guaranteed paid family leave, they do not have equal rights.

When women, especially women of color, receive less in wages than their male counterparts, they do not have equal rights.

Friend, we as a nation cannot strip women of their health care autonomy, force them to choose between raising their children or continuing their career, pay them less, and claim that they have equal rights in the United States.

Last year, I was an original cosponsor of the Women's Health Protection Act of 2022, the Equal Access to Abortion Coverage in Health Insurance Act, and the Paycheck Fairness Act to ensure women across the country received the equal treatment they deserve.

But while my Democratic colleagues and I continue trying to pave a path forward for equity, our GOP majority continues to do nothing but push incendiary culture wars, spread misinformation, and roll back fundamental rights.

Enough is enough.

With a majority in the House, the GOP now has the power to block any legislation to advance the economic, social, and political rights of women. That’s why my colleagues and I are already gearing up to take back the House in 2024: We will continue fighting for and expanding the rights of women in every aspect of society.

If you’re ready to join our fight to take back our majority and work to build back a better and truly balanced world for women across the US, please make a contribution in honor of International Women’s Day today. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ESCA-14-Email-March2023-FR-March?refcode=E20526-20230308-InternationalWomensDay-SL-Holiday-RegTemplate-NA-NA&amount=5]

DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ESCA-14-Email-March2023-FR-March?refcode=E20526-20230308-InternationalWomensDay-SB-Holiday-RegTemplate-NA-NA&amount=5]

As a father to a fiercely independent daughter, I want more than anything to see her grow up in a country that doesn’t interfere with her personal, private health care decisions. Should she one day choose to have a child of her own, I want her to be a parent and have a successful career. And I want to make sure she doesn’t receive a cent less than her male counterparts.

https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ESCA-14-Email-March2023-FR-March?refcode=E20526-20230308-InternationalWomensDay-graphic-Holiday-RegTemplate-NA-NA&amount=5 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ESCA-14-Email-March2023-FR-March?refcode=E20526-20230308-InternationalWomensDay-graphic-Holiday-RegTemplate-NA-NA&amount=5]

This is only a fraction of the work we need to do to ensure our daughters and our granddaughters grow up in a world that treats them as equals. It won’t be easy, but it is necessary.

Together, we can and will accomplish this.

— Eric
DONATE TO ERIC >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ESCA-14-Email-march2023-fr-march?refcode=headerfooter-footer&amount=25]
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ESCA-14-Email-march2023-fr-march?refcode=headerfooter-footer&amount=25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ESCA-14-Email-march2023-fr-march?refcode=headerfooter-footer&amount=25]
Text ERIC to 43033 to get text updates! On your phone? Press here to sign up in just two clicks >> [http://go.txting.io/shfylh] Democrat Eric Swalwell proudly represents California's 14th Congressional District. He needs your support in order to keep protecting what's important: our healthcare, our paychecks, our future, and our democracy.
DONATE TO ERIC >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ESCA-14-Email-march2023-fr-march?refcode=headerfooter-footer&amount=25]
Eric runs a people-powered campaign that relies on grassroots supporters like you, and if he's going to win re-election, he's going to need your help. If you'd still like to stop receiving emails, you can unsubscribe: https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/sSRynqGvEUuAD1QP3hBpsA2?unsubscribedata=RjdI4yN43nqCnhgUixxl0QCnDs%2BSN5XA8UzP%2FsKnlcpCUmwi%2B15nRqwOPi5YjwafnhaLOMb7hcu9979bAgNlUXBHyITzeNMGY6Fv5OkCtfstuSzU9YwrMl4ECWLCG1TM4UxX3MJx7cwRnPaWdMf9vOM%2FpDqtR8yZqJ4jOzJc9KF7TLdZOoip0LI7Fj4yazA8s1K94%2FBRjzkZWUcGD577g%2FgpzfCytOvEpNqD6YOyW6Ph%2Bjz9XKEpI5KZHIznsXmVr2EXiEj%2FYSWgY0hxCZ7%2FdhecQtfKVcTPReo9lsL6Ak%2BSiuodnqjAlRNA3fKsSABQRzVmvGLcwbFs30pCOHfvAO%2Fy6FjEq428mWKhKzY1vLIfKuoGFAKFN5YEHPPWELX5wuZdMQbjG9wmjDRN1GM7yqk5IOQFQKdN9orvUgin8fQ%3D

name: Sherrod Brown
subject: Reaching out personally
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH |
body:
The battle for Senate control could all come down to Ohio
| | |
---
Hi, friend, it’s Sen. Sherrod Brown from Ohio. I’ve spent my life fighting for the Dignity of Work – and I mean _all_ work. Whether you punch a time clock or swipe a badge, earn a salary or make tips, are raising children or caring for an aging parent, I’ll fight for you.
I’m running for reelection so I can _keep_ fighting for workers and families in the Senate – but I need your help. As the only remaining statewide Democrat in Ohio, analysts at CNN say I’m one of the most vulnerable senators on the ballot – and national Republicans are already launching attack ads against me.
**The battle for Senate control could all come down to Ohio – so please, I’m personally asking:[Will you give $3 or more right now – split between my campaign and Maggie Hassan’s – to help defend this battleground seat and keep the Senate blue? ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59886074/395377484/-1928913949?amounts=3,10,25,50,100&refcode=mh-jtk2205-bu-nd&recurring=0&amount=3&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODg1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkOGE1YWQwMC1mMGJkLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DXNbEXnhuGYXiZkm0ThzVp0vxmz_Pn9BtGpjBYD2YL0=)**
I grew up in Mansfield, Ohio, in a community of ironworkers, carpenters, autoworkers, and electricians. And I’ll be honest with you: The Ohio I live in now looks a lot different than the one I grew up in. I’m fighting in the Senate every day to bring those jobs back, to build our economy by investing in the middle class, and to ensure hard work pays off for every American.
But I’m facing my toughest race yet – in a state Donald Trump won by 8 points. Last year’s Ohio Senate race was one of the most costly and competitive in the nation, and NBC News reports that this one could be “even nastier and more expensive.”
**The long and short of it is, I can’t win – and Democrats simply can’t hold onto our narrow Senate majority – if we don’t have strong grassroots support from folks like you.**
**[That’s why I’m asking for your support today, friend: Please, will you chip in $3 or more now – split between my campaign and Maggie Hassan’s – so we can win in Ohio and defend the Senate?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59886076/395377486/-1928913949?amounts=3,10,25,50,100&refcode=mh-jtk2205-bu-nd&recurring=0&amount=3&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODg1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkOGE1YWQwMC1mMGJkLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DXNbEXnhuGYXiZkm0ThzVp0vxmz_Pn9BtGpjBYD2YL0=)**
With gratitude,
Sherrod Brown
| If you'd like to support Maggie Hassan for Senate, **[donate now](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59886078/395377488/-1928913949?amounts=3,10,25,50,100&refcode=mh-jtk2205-bu-nd&recurring=0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODg1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkOGE1YWQwMC1mMGJkLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DXNbEXnhuGYXiZkm0ThzVp0vxmz_Pn9BtGpjBYD2YL0=)**. name: Team WFP
subject: Republicans out. Working Families in.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Working Families Party National PAC (77 Sands St
body:
| | | Peter, In Philadelphia, there’s a city council race that could legitimately shock America’s two-party system. **If you haven’t heard already: Nicolas O’Rourke, a pastor and longtime organizer, is running on the Working Families Party ballot line** to oust one of the last remaining Republicans on Philadelphia’s City Council — and make the Working Families Party the _second-largest_ party in Philadelphia. Now, Nic is one-step closer to booting the Republican Party out of Philadelphia’s City Hall after David Oh, the 10-year incumbent Republican he was running against resigned to run for mayor — leaving an open seat. **This election is going to come down to just how many voters we reach between now and Election Day — because when voters know about Nic and his vision for a Philadelphia that works for all of us, they support him.[Click here to watch our 60-second interview with Nicolas O’Rourke, and then, if you're inspired by his message, split a contribution between his campaign and PA WFP to clean_body: | | | Peter, In Philadelphia, there’s a city council race that could legitimately shock America’s two-party system. If you haven’t heard already: Nicolas O’Rourke, a pastor and longtime organizer, is running on the Working Families Party ballot line to oust one of the last remaining Republicans on Philadelphia’s City Council — and make the Working Families Party the second-largest party in Philadelphia. Now, Nic is one-step closer to booting the Republican Party out of Philadelphia’s City Hall after David Oh, the 10-year incumbent Republican he was running against resigned to run for mayor — leaving an open seat. This election is going to come down to just how many voters we reach between now and Election Day — because when voters know about Nic and his vision for a Philadelphia that works for all of us, they support him. Nic’s campaign slogan is Republicans out. Working Families in. — and this means two things: 1. In Philadelphia, the City Council reserves two at-large seats for the minority party, which has historically been the Republican Party. That is, until we shocked the political establishment in 2019 by electing Kendra Brooks to one of those two seats on the Working Families Party ballot line. If Nic and Kendra both win their races against their Republican opponents, they would effectively end Republican governing power on the Council, and the Working Families Party would ascend as a viable second party in Philadelphia’s City Council. 2. For decades, Republicans have used the two at-large seats reserved for the minority party to block progress on the issues that matter most to working families. They’ve voted on everything from siphoning public money out of public schools to giving big developers tax breaks while workers and families are left at the mercy of the housing crisis. With a Working Families Party minority on the city council, we can push the Democratic Party in a more progressive direction and deliver on priorities for working families. If you’re ready to kick Republicans out of one of the largest cities in the country: We're pushing for voters to do something not many have done before — vote for a new party. And we need the resources to reach folks across Philadelphia, introduce them to Nic, explain why his election is game-changing for working families, and win in November. In solidarity, Team WFP Thoughts? Feedback? We want to hear it. Reply directly to this email and let us know. This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you received this email as a forward and want to get the latest updates from the Working Families Party directly, The Working Families Party is the party of the multiracial working class, fighting for a nation that cares for all of us. Wanna learn more? And if you need to update your contact information, Other political groups have corporate donors. But we can pool our resources too. Paid for by Working Families Party National PAC (77 Sands St. #6, Brooklyn, NY, 11201). | | Sent via . name: Geoff Simpson
subject: An insider update on our candidate recruitment 👀
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Hi, Peter! I wanted
to personally reach out to share an update on our candidate recruitment
efforts for this 2024 cycle.

But first, I want to thank you for being a part of this organization. We
don’t take a dime from corporate PACs, so your support means a whole lot
to us, especially now as it’s a tough time financially for many grassroots
organizations and nonprofits.

Now, to share some updates about what we’re focused on right now. We have
been laser focused on candidate recruitment:

* Last cycle, we spoke with 3,990 local leaders about our races and
vetted 1,653 potential candidates to ensure we backed the best
possible leaders. This is what we are in the middle of doing right
now!

But, unfortunately, right when our work and need for resources is ramping
up – donations to JD have started to slow down. Our work right now depends
on our ability to fundraise in order to execute on our ambitious plans.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/130140?refcode=em20230308-27884&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1001&akid=27884%2E1203867%2EZXuIWt ]Will you rush a $3 contribution right now to support our
candidate recruitment efforts?

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/130140?refcode=em20230308-27884&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1020&akid=27884%2E1203867%2EZXuIWt ]Contribute $3

AOC in 2018; Jamaal Bowman and Cori Bush in 2020; and most recently Summer
Lee in 2022 all came out of our candidate recruitment program. And
everyday we are talking to potential candidates who could join them and
the rest of the squad in 2024!

But it can only happen if we are able to reach our fundraising goals.

We are $20,535 away from hitting our mid-goal this month and asking
everyone who cares about JD’s existence to chip in whatever they can.
[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/129870?refcode=em20230308-27884&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1021&akid=27884%2E1203867%2EZXuIWt ]Will you make your best gift right now?

Thank you so much for your support,

Geoff Simpson
Campaigns Director

Do not worry if you cannot afford to make a contribution — we understand that
this is a difficult time. If you’re struggling, you can find a food bank
[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/28883?t=1022&akid=27884%2E1203867%2EZXuIWt ]here. We appreciate everything you do to keep our movement strong.

Please stay informed and follow the most up-to-date recommendations from the
[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/24438?t=1023&akid=27884%2E1203867%2EZXuIWt ]Centers for Disease Control and your state public health department.

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20230308-27884-footer&t=1024&refcode2=27884_1203867_ZXuIWt&akid=27884%2E1203867%2EZXuIWt

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1025&akid=27884%2E1203867%2EZXuIWt

PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.
JusticeDemocrats.com
10629 Hardin Valley Rd, #226
Knoxville, TN 37932
Email us: us@justicedemocrats.com

name: Jennifer Wexton
subject: A day to recommit to the fight for gender equality
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Wexton for Congress
body:
From restoring the right to choose, to achieving pay equity, there is so much more to be done.

[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59886327/395378433/-772585877?refcode=EM_FR_230308IWD_N_JW_FULL_4_NG_N_Logo&fn=&ln=&em=dpwillis67@gmail.com&add1=&ci=Cresco&st=PA&pc=18326&oc=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjOWI0NzBiNS1mMGJkLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=wSmv5CzvrC9lEMGa-FNz92L-ZMSy4bgMK9w72CSftRU=)

Today is International Women's Day — a day to celebrate the achievements of women and recommit to realizing gender equality.

I was first elected to the House as part of a historic wave of women in 2018. Since entering Congress, I've supported legislation that expands women’s access to quality, affordable health care, family planning services, and contraception. I've voted to expand paid family and medical leave programs, end violence against women, promote women’s involvement in STEM and political fields, and close the gender pay gap.

But there is much more to be done. The last few years have shown unmistakably how women are disproportionately impacted by economic and healthcare disparities. And in the United States over the last 8 months, tens of millions of women have had to adjust to a stark new reality in which their right to reproductive freedom is no longer recognized.

I will keep fighting to restore the right to choose — and I'll keep fighting for gender equality on _all_ fronts. [**My advocacy is likely to make me a top target of anti-choice Republicans (just as it did in 2022), so if you can chip in any amount today to my re-election campaign, it would make a huge difference. Your grassroots support is what fuels our continued advocacy and our ability to fight back against right-wing misinformation.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59886328/395378434/-772585877?refcode=EM_FR_230308IWD_N_JW_FULL_4_NG_N_L1&fn=&ln=&em=dpwillis67@gmail.com&add1=&ci=Cresco&st=PA&pc=18326&oc=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjOWI0NzBiNS1mMGJkLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=wSmv5CzvrC9lEMGa-FNz92L-ZMSy4bgMK9w72CSftRU=) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | **[$10 »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/59886329/395378435/-772585877?express_lane=true&refcode=EM_FR_230308IWD_N_JW_FULL_4_NG_N_EB10&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjOWI0NzBiNS1mMGJkLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=wSmv5CzvrC9lEMGa-FNz92L-ZMSy4bgMK9w72CSftRU=)**

subject: The fight isn’t over. The fight has only just begun.

The Democratic rule is officially over, and house Republicans have promised to hold the out-of-touch Democrats accountable by launching major investigations. YET, this won’t stop the Democratic-controlled Senate from taking advantage of their majority and passing restrictive gun bills that harm law-abiding gun owners like **YOU** – as they continue to do nothing to prevent the rising crime rates facing our major cities.

name: Wiley Nickel
subject: On International Women’s Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Wiley Nickel for Congress
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2303-q1-wn?refcode=230308-email [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2303-q1-wn?refcode=230308-email]Friends, On International Women’s Day, we celebrate the social, economic, cultural and political achievements of women. I’d like to take the opportunity to highlight one of the many women who has made history in the state of North Carolina. In 1920, Lillian Exum Clement became the first woman elected to the North Carolina House of Representatives. Not only did she make history in our state, but this victory marked the first time a woman was elected to serve in any state legislature in the US. In addition to her career as a politician, Clement was the first woman in North Carolina to open a law practice without male law firm partners. Clement broke glass ceiling after glass ceiling, and today we celebrate the progress she made for generations of women. Last year, when Roe v. Wade was overturned, I saw the rights of my daughter and other young women rolled back decades. I ran for Congress to ensure a better future for our children. A future where women can make their own choices about their bodies without the government interfering. A future where women are paid fairly and have the same opportunities as their male counterparts. A future where women have access and can afford the health care they need. Today, as we celebrate the accomplishments of women around the world, I recommit myself to this important work. Thank you so much, WIley Nickel Wiley Nickel is the Congressman for NC-13. His 2022 opponent, Bo Hines, has announced he'll run against Wiley in 2024. Support #TeamWiley with a monthly donation today! [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2302-q1-wn?refcode=230228-email>I hope you can contribute toward the campaign so we can rebuild our warchest and hire our first staffer for the cycle.

name: Replace Sinema
subject: Peter, this dark money front group could guaranteeSinema’s nomination
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Change for Arizona 2024 PAC
body:
Dark money front group No Labels qualifies for ballot access in AZ | |
---
Peter – BREAKING: No Labels, a dark money front group for Wall Street and GOP billionaires, has qualified for ballot access in Arizona — which could ultimately undermine Democrats up and down the ticket, including the Senate race AND President Biden’s chances of reelection. When Kyrsten Sinema undermined President Biden’s legislative agenda and blocked priorities like paid family leave, No Labels ran TV ads praising her. When Sinema worked to protect Wall Street and billionaires, No Labels called her "heroic". Now, we have no doubt that No Labels will do everything they can to encourage Kyrsten Sinema to stay in the race and block a Democrat from winning — all while they continue to pad her campaign account with corporate money. Meanwhile, analysts are already saying that No Labels pursuing third party status in battleground states puts President Biden’s re-election at risk and could help put Donald Trump or Ron DeSantis over the top.
No Labels tries to portray itself as independent from both parties, but it’s actually just a Republican dark money operation intent on undermining Democrats. They keep their donors secret, but their known backers include billionaire private equity investors and Trump mega-donors. **Sinema has received over $100,000 in donations**(that we know of) from No Labels donors and their spouses.
Sinema didn’t leave the Democratic party so she could be ‘an independent voice for Arizona.’ **She left because knew she could better serve her corporate donors outside the party.**

clean_body: Dark money front group No Labels qualifies for ballot access in AZ | | Peter – BREAKING: No Labels, a dark money front group for Wall Street and GOP billionaires, has qualified for ballot access in Arizona — which could ultimately undermine Democrats up and down the ticket, including the Senate race AND President Biden’s chances of reelection. When Kyrsten Sinema undermined President Biden’s legislative agenda and blocked priorities like paid family leave, No Labels ran TV ads praising her. When Sinema worked to protect Wall Street and billionaires, No Labels called her "heroic". Now, we have no doubt that No Labels will do everything they can to encourage Kyrsten Sinema to stay in the race and block a Democrat from winning — all while they continue to pad her campaign account with corporate money. This is why we’re doing the work to make sure Ruben Gallego, or whoever ends up with the Democratic nomination, has all the resources they need to beat her in 2024. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately: | | | | | | | | | | Meanwhile, analysts are already saying that No Labels pursuing third party status in battleground states puts President Biden’s re-election at risk and could help put Donald Trump or Ron DeSantis over the top. No Labels tries to portray itself as independent from both parties, but it’s actually just a Republican dark money operation intent on undermining Democrats. They keep their donors secret, but their known backers include billionaire private equity investors and Trump mega-donors. Sinema has received over $100,000 in donations(that we know of) from No Labels donors and their spouses. Sinema didn’t leave the Democratic party so she could be ‘an independent voice for Arizona.’ She left because knew she could better serve her corporate donors outside the party. for all your help, \- Replace Sinema & Change for Arizona 2024 Paid for by Change for Arizona 2024 PAC. | | Sent via . subject: These three days will impact make-or-break Senate races, Peter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by EMILYs List [www
body:
|
---
| | We're raising $150,000 to Save the Senate in 2024. Are you in? [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/59886717/395379623/2061439929?sourceid=1048729&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YjU4NzMyMS1mMmJkLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dXBfsGLv_zU3Ut9Jb2ANVK23yFv68dWTFzQjQXDCRPA=&emci=02e9b242-ebbd-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=5b587321-f2bd-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=5177934) Today's the first day of our Save the Senate Fund — a three-day push to help power the campaigns of Democratic pro-choice women committed to saving our razor-thin Senate majority this cycle. **Donate on Launch Day to invest early in what will be make-or-break Senate races to save reproductive access nationwide:**

**[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/59886718/395379624/2061439929?sourceid=1048729&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YjU4NzMyMS1mMmJkLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dXBfsGLv_zU3Ut9Jb2ANVK23yFv68dWTFzQjQXDCRPA=&emci=02e9b242-ebbd-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=5b587321-f2bd-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=5177934)** I'll be blunt, Peter: Our Senate majority is _razor-thin_.

While we successfully grew the Senate majority beyond a 50-50 tie last November, _just_ _two seats_ stand between us and a Republican-controlled Congress. And there is no question what a Republican Senate would prioritize — we're already seeing the GOP's extreme anti-choice agenda playing out in the House.

Which is why I wanted to reach out on the Launch Day of our three-day Save the Senate Fund. We've set an **ambitious goal of $150,000** to invest in Democratic pro-choice women who will fight this cycle to keep the Senate gavel out of the hands of anti-choice Republicans. We're thinking of this as a down payment on the success of the women we'll support this cycle, and we _know_ that they'll pay dividends in their fierce pro-choice leadership! **[Are you in, Peter? Donate now, on the *first day* of our Save the Senate Fund, to help send the message loud and clear: The race for the Senate majority is a marathon, not a sprint, and we're not ceding a single inch to anti-choice Republicans.](https://click.everyaction.com/k/59886729/395379637/2061439929?sourceid=1048729&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YjU4NzMyMS1mMmJkLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dXBfsGLv_zU3Ut9Jb2ANVK23yFv68dWTFzQjQXDCRPA=&emci=02e9b242-ebbd-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=5b587321-f2bd-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=5177934)** House Republicans have been hard at work attacking abortion access since Kevin McCarthy received the speaker's gavel, and have already passed _two anti-choice bills since mid-January_. Without the federal protections of _Roe v. Wade_ in place, House Republicans _will_ pass a national abortion ban — and the only thing stopping these bans from heading to the president's desk is the tiny Democratic Senate majority. The first quarter of the 2023-2024 cycle may seem early to begin raising the warning bells, but I know that you've seen the numbers, Peter. Democrats are defending 23 Senate seats, _over twice the amount_ that Republicans will have to defend, just to maintain our two-seat advantage. Anti-choice Republicans are already launching (and bankrolling) campaigns with the hopes of flipping as many seats as possible — so this is where we fight back. ********We know what we're up against, so it's time we roll up our sleeves and get to work. Join the fight to Save the Senate by donating toward our $150,000 goal now:**

**[https://emilyslist.org/go/Save-The-Senate](https://click.everyaction.com/k/59886730/395379638/2061439929?sourceid=1048729&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YjU4NzMyMS1mMmJkLWVkMTEtYThlMC0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dXBfsGLv_zU3Ut9Jb2ANVK23yFv68dWTFzQjQXDCRPA=&emci=02e9b242-ebbd-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&emdi=5b587321-f2bd-ed11-a8e0-00224832e811&ceid=5177934)******** Thank you, Laphonza Butler
President, EMILYs List

clean_body: | | | We're raising $150,000 to Save the Senate in 2024. Are you in? Today's the first day of our Save the Senate Fund — a three-day push to help power the campaigns of Democratic pro-choice women committed to saving our razor-thin Senate majority this cycle. Donate on Launch Day to invest early in what will be make-or-break Senate races to save reproductive access nationwide: I'll be blunt, Peter: Our Senate majority is razor-thin. While we successfully grew the Senate majority beyond a 50-50 tie last November, just two seats stand between us and a Republican-controlled Congress. And there is no question what a Republican Senate would prioritize — we're already seeing the GOP's extreme anti-choice agenda playing out in the House. Which is why I wanted to reach out on the Launch Day of our three-day Save the Senate Fund. We've set an ambitious goal of $150,000 to invest in Democratic pro-choice women who will fight this cycle to keep the Senate gavel out of the hands of anti-choice Republicans. We're thinking of this as a down payment on the success of the women we'll support this cycle, and we know that they'll pay dividends in their fierce pro-choice leadership! House Republicans have been hard at work attacking abortion access since Kevin McCarthy received the speaker's gavel, and have already passed two anti-choice bills since mid-January. Without the federal protections of Roe v. Wade in place, House Republicans will pass a national abortion ban — and the only thing stopping these bans from heading to the president's desk is the tiny Democratic Senate majority. The first quarter of the 2023-2024 cycle may seem early to begin raising the warning bells, but I know that you've seen the numbers, Peter. Democrats are defending 23 Senate seats, over twice the amount that Republicans will have to defend, just to maintain our two-seat advantage. Anti-choice Republicans are already launching (and bankrolling) campaigns with the hopes of flipping as many seats as possible — so this is where we fight back. ******We know what we're up against, so it's time we roll up our sleeves and get to work. Join the fight to Save the Senate by donating toward our $150,000 goal now: **** Thank you, Laphonza Butler President, EMILYs List ****** | ---|---|--- EMILYs List is a community of over five million members that helps elect Democratic pro-choice women to office. | Paid for by EMILYs List and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. name: Defend the Constitution
subject: Access “C.S. Lewis on Christianity”
body:
C.S. Lewis was a master at addressing life's most important questions through vivid language that is delightful and captivating to read.

| | | This sponsored message from Defend The Constitution is on behalf of Hillsdale College [](https://links.defend-the-constitution.org/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=4838f69d36cf4d8593ce8810b3ff475c&_e=NYfB6GoE7WIOAzflCSz9dJejWQn6UKNIPG8uXk9vBJrNtbNWvYS29r60JnrFuhkb7ZwQk_AutXk78HfwZ9q7EesTo_ZqujqT9lUS4LNECQDz5eFc9uxXRN5ppY2G8bXoLZMM5xhl6OzKdUhdwKfOeYoI_83ZSPL0wib1By1FpPTfyu-9Utsx82v2zlWagdur1QoAtxOGuQ7HEXvQ68BPpOF9orvJWye-cLl2VYM0mbtU77_fnRYF52FHnttdBAKzHFElLrinO5cXEsOXbJG8Hh88xQ1hk33913jLvZDc3XFwcznoGFWcRm59zNUGOLzWertOhX3TxZFZiYHbpeItEifNrNYyi6H_mcJE80xO6O2J2awfwbnX-s99FcurUWkvheJxj0WLqTMT9FRIEMYqn9mNphxTfo0KkqbkQNq61bf3GRRGDKdaR6TIS2N1H5gA)
